summary: Iteration 2 E2E review of HRMS new modules (Payroll, Performance, Recruitment, Reports, Settings). Backend: 30/31 pytest tests passed (96.8%). Frontend: admin sidebar, payroll admin page, reports page (6 KPI cards + 4 charts), settings page (4 sections), admin/manager performance page (3 sliders + reviews table), recruiter console, recruiter jobs list, employee payslips list/detail, employee performance (overall card + trend line + goals progress bars) all render and integrate correctly. One backend spec mismatch found: /api/v1/dashboard/recruiter returns `interviews_this_week` instead of `interviews` per spec.

minor.issue: Spec asked for key `interviews` but API returns `interviews_this_week`. Either align spec or rename key. Other keys (open_jobs, total_candidates, pipeline, recent_jobs) all present and correct.

issues: Repeated console warning: ResponsiveContainer width(-1) height(-1) — chart height/width is not set on initial mount. Cosmetic; charts render after layout. Add minHeight to wrapper to silence.

action_items: Decide on /api/v1/dashboard/recruiter response key: either rename `interviews_this_week` -> `interviews` (matches spec & UI may already consume one of them) or update spec.
Optional: silence ResponsiveContainer width(-1) warnings by setting a min-height on chart wrappers (e.g. minHeight: 300).
critical_code_review_comments: payroll.py generate is correctly idempotent (re-runs return existing rows; commits per-loop avoided thanks to single commit + flush per new row). Good.

recruitment.py POST /jobs/{id}/apply correctly has NO auth dependency — public endpoint works as required (verified via curl-equivalent in pytest, status 201 with no Authorization header). Also creates notifications for all hr_recruiter users and correctly returns 409 if job is closed.
performance.py overall_score correctly computed as avg(goals,skills,attitude) rounded to 2dp. Goal status auto-transitions (>=100 → completed, 0<p<100 → in_progress) verified.
performance.py team_reviews correctly scopes senior_manager to direct reports via Employee.manager_id; admin sees all.

reports.py /overview returns all required keys (total_employees, total_departments, open_jobs, total_candidates, approved_leaves, pending_leaves, headcount_by_department, leave_by_type, top_performers, payroll_recent, candidate_pipeline). Properly admin-only.
candidate status update validates enum membership (400 for invalid stage like 'bogus_stage').
Frontend Settings page is client-side only (mocked) — backend persistence intentionally deferred per spec; UI clearly shows Profile/Notifications/Workweek/Integrations sections.
Sidebar correctly shows for admin: Dashboard, Employees, Departments, Attendance, Leave Approvals, Payroll, Performance, Recruitment, Reports, Settings — all 10 links rendered.
Recruiter dashboard pipeline breakdown shows 5 stages with counts correctly (Offered/Applied/Interview/Screened/Rejected).

success_rate.frontend: ~98% (all critical flows render & integrate; one cosmetic chart warning)

seed_data_creation: Test created: payroll for Jan/2026 (8 employees, idempotent), 1 review (admin->Avery Stone), 2 TEST_ goals (employee1), 2 TEST_ jobs (TEST_Engineer open, TEST_Closed closed), 2 TEST_ candidates (test_public@example.com, test_publicapply2@example.com on Senior Frontend Engineer). One existing candidate flipped to 'interview' status.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest suites now exist: backend_test.py (iteration 1, 37 tests) and test_iteration2.py (iteration 2, 31 tests). Run all: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/all_results.xml`. Public apply endpoint POST /jobs/{id}/apply is PUBLIC — works without Authorization header (verified). Kanban page at /recruiter/candidates renders 5 stages (Applied/Screened/Interview/Offered/Rejected) and cards are draggable=true. Public /apply/:jobId route renders without auth — verified the route is reachable; the form submission flow via UI was not E2E verified due to a Playwright fetch caching issue but backend endpoint is confirmed working. Employee payslip detail uses labels like 'Provident Fund' / 'Tax' / 'Other Deductions' / 'Net Salary' (not 'PF' / 'Net') — adjust frontend tests accordingly. Top of payslip shows allowances (HRA/Transport/Medical) and Gross. Single backend assertion failure was a key-name spec mismatch (interviews vs interviews_this_week) — not a functional bug.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA for recruiter dashboard key naming: backend returns {open_jobs, total_candidates, interviews_this_week, pipeline, recent_jobs} while spec/review_request lists 'interviews'. Reproduction: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <recruiter>' /api/v1/dashboard/recruiter -> response contains interviews_this_week (value=2). Mitigation: rename key in app/api/routes/dashboard.py recruiter_dashboard handler from interviews_this_week -> interviews, OR keep both for backward compat. The frontend recruiter console correctly renders 'IN INTERVIEW: 2' card so the UI is likely already consuming the existing key — confirm before renaming.
